title: Phrynium casupo
intro: Phrynium calophytum, also known as Phrynium calophytum, is a tropical plant species belonging to the family Marantaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Phrynium calophytum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal preparations, Phrynium calophytum is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, which are used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and inflammation.
